nodes.title: Climate Action Framework Act

body.processed:
The Climate Action Framework Act establishes legally binding emissions reduction targets and creates an institutional framework for coordinating the nation's response to climate change.

Key Provisions

  • Net-zero greenhouse gas emissions target by 2050
  • Interim target of 55% reduction by 2035 (from 2005 levels)
  • Establishment of an independent Climate Action Commission
  • Five-year carbon budgets with sectoral allocations
  • Green transition fund of 2 billion ALDs for renewable energy and efficiency

Legislative History

The bill was introduced by the Prime Minister in response to growing public concern about climate change and the recommendations of the National Climate Advisory Panel.
body.summary: The Climate Action Framework Act establishes legally binding emissions reduction targets and creates an institutional framework for coordinating the nation's response to climate change.Key ProvisionsN

nodes.committeeReferral: Standing Committee on Environment and Natural Resources

body.processed:
The Digital Privacy Protection Bill strengthens data privacy rights for citizens and establishes new obligations for organizations that collect, process, and store personal data.

Key Provisions

  • Right to data portability and the right to be forgotten
  • Mandatory data breach notification within 72 hours
  • Restrictions on automated decision-making affecting individuals
  • Establishment of a Data Protection Commissioner with enforcement powers
  • Penalties of up to 4% of annual revenue for non-compliance

Committee Review

The Select Committee on Digital Innovation held three public hearings on the bill, receiving testimony from technology companies, civil liberties organizations, and academic experts.
body.summary: The Digital Privacy Protection Bill strengthens data privacy rights for citizens and establishes new obligations for organizations that collect, process, and store personal data.Key ProvisionsRight to

nodes.title: Education Modernization and Access Act

body.processed:
The Education Modernization and Access Act reforms the national education system to improve quality, expand access, and prepare students for the demands of the modern economy.

Key Provisions

  • Universal pre-kindergarten for all children ages 3-4
  • Updated national curriculum emphasizing STEM and digital literacy
  • Teacher recruitment and retention incentive program
  • Expanded vocational training and apprenticeship pathways
  • Student loan reform with income-based repayment

Impact Assessment

The Parliamentary Budget Office estimates the reforms will cost approximately 1.8 billion ALDs annually, funded through a reallocation of existing education budget and new revenue from the Digital Services Tax.
body.summary: The Education Modernization and Access Act reforms the national education system to improve quality, expand access, and prepare students for the demands of the modern economy.Key ProvisionsUniversal p
